bowed.note: /baUd/ rhymes with loud (bend-at-waist sense), NOT /boUd/ like code

Luthan.note: SOFT / voiceless th (the sound in 'thin', 'breath') — NOT the hard/voiced th of 'the'. Operator ear-confirmed 2026-07-14: the current value renders correctly.
Luthan.source: canon: the-war-for-paratua/audiobook/book1/lexicon.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (soft th is correct)

Luthans.note: SOFT / voiceless th (the sound in 'thin', 'breath') — NOT the hard/voiced th of 'the'. Operator ear-confirmed 2026-07-14: the current value renders correctly.
Luthans.source: canon: the-war-for-paratua/audiobook/book1/lexicon.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (soft th is correct)

Secrulaur.note: sek-ru-LAY-ar. Heriarch, Lord of Light; made the Monodent; died at the Challenge of Final Fall. The 'sek' spelling is load-bearing: a leading 'sec' invites a soft-c reading.
Secrulaur.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (supersedes 'sec-RU-lay-ar' 2026-07-13, which was stress notation wrongly placed in say and was spoken literally; and the earlier 'Sekrulear')

Quasition.note: kwuh-ZID-ee-an: SHORT i, stress on the second syllable. The Great Druid, last Ward, keeper of the Wall of Wills. Spaced form (cf. 'Ra Veena', 'Bree two') because Kokoro reads any 'quasi-' spelling as the English word quasi (KWAY-zye) and gives a long I; the doubled d closes the syllable and holds the i short. The earlier 'KWA-zit-ee-on' first-syllable target is SUPERSEDED by the operator's ear.
Quasition.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (supersedes 'KWA-zit-ee-on' 2026-07-13, which was stress notation wrongly placed in say and was spoken literally; and 'Quasidian', which had the right shape but a long I)

Tuesasche.note: too-sash-AI: the ending is an AYE sound, NOT the 'ah' the 2026-07-11 entry assumed. Jherassin leader; holds the neutral peace of the Cirshe under-city warrens. The old ipa (tuːˈsæʃɑː, ending ɑː) contradicted the operator's ear and was cleared rather than guessed at; re-derive from the confirmed render if an ipa is wanted. Possessive needs no separate entry: the whole-word matcher yields "Too sashai's" (verified 2026-07-14 against src/lexicon.mjs, 18 possessives in Book I).
Tuesasche.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (supersedes 'too-SASH-ah' 2026-07-11, which was stress notation wrongly placed in say and was spoken literally; this name had no correct prior rendering)

Dariticus.note: deh-RID-i-cus, stress on the second syllable. Order of Twelve; Tower of Dariticus; Crysalis on Tolaris. The earlier 'DAR-it-ic-us' first-syllable target is SUPERSEDED by the operator's ear.
Dariticus.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 — RESTORES the pre-2026-07-13 'Deridicus'. The 2026-07-13 'DAR-it-ic-us' was stress notation wrongly placed in say and was spoken literally.

breath.note: NOUN /brEth/, rhymes with death. Operator ear-confirmed 2026-07-14.
breath.source: canon: the-war-for-paratua/audiobook/book1/lexicon.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (noun)

breaths.note: NOUN /brEth/, rhymes with death. Operator ear-confirmed 2026-07-14.
breaths.source: canon: the-war-for-paratua/audiobook/book1/lexicon.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (noun)

Breath.note: sentence-initial noun (case-sensitive matcher needs the capitalized key); parity with base-en.json NOUN /brEth/, rhymes with death. Operator ear-confirmed 2026-07-14.
Breath.source: canon: voice-engine/lexicons/base-en.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (noun)

Breaths.note: sentence-initial noun; parity with base-en.json NOUN /brEth/, rhymes with death. Operator ear-confirmed 2026-07-14.
Breaths.source: canon: voice-engine/lexicons/base-en.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (noun)

breathe.note: VERB, long-ee voiced-th (say 'breethe', rhymes with 'seethe'). Operator A/B-confirmed 2026-06-24 on Kokoro bm_george. Re-added 2026-07-09; the 2026-06-26 removal was in error.
breathe.source: canon: voice-engine/lexicons/base-en.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (infinitive / participle)

breathed.note: VERB PAST TENSE. HARD/VOICED th (the sound in 'the', 'breathe', 'seethe') — NOT the soft/voiceless th of 'breath'. The apostrophe preserves the magic-e that voices the th: plain 'breethed' loses it and the engine renders a SOFT th, which the operator rejected 2026-07-14. Do not 'clean up' the apostrophe.
breathed.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (supersedes 'breethed', which carried an unearned status:locked inherited from the old book1 lexicon and rendered a voiceless th). 24 uses in Book I.

breathing.source: canon: voice-engine/lexicons/base-en.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (infinitive / participle)

Breathe.source: canon: voice-engine/lexicons/base-en.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (infinitive / participle)

Breathed.note: VERB PAST TENSE. HARD/VOICED th (the sound in 'the', 'breathe', 'seethe') — NOT the soft/voiceless th of 'breath'. The apostrophe preserves the magic-e that voices the th: plain 'breethed' loses it and the engine renders a SOFT th, which the operator rejected 2026-07-14. Do not 'clean up' the apostrophe. Sentence-initial form.
Breathed.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (supersedes 'breethed', which carried an unearned status:locked inherited from the old book1 lexicon and rendered a voiceless th). 24 uses in Book I.

Breathing.source: canon: voice-engine/lexicons/base-en.json; EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (infinitive / participle)

Atar.note: The word ATE + TAR, said as ONE word. The hyphen is a WORD-JOINER, not stress notation: Kokoro reads 'Ate-tar' as a single token, which is what wins here. Spaced 'Ate tar' has the right vowel but the engine pauses inside the name (6.35s vs 5.93s in identical text, measured 2026-07-14); plain 'Atetar' closes the gap but loses the vowel. The hyphen gets both. WARNING: the old 'Aytar' rendered as 'Itar' — the 'ay' digraph collapses to a long-I on this voice. Do not reintroduce it.
Atar.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (supersedes 'Aytar', which carried status:confirmed from 2026-06-24 but was NEVER ear-checked and mispronounced the name as 'Itar' in every render since June; 227 uses across 14 chapters of Book I)

eat.note: long-E /iːt/, NOT 'ate' — operator caught the TTS saying 'ate' (queen's dinner line, Ch 1)

ten.note: The number-word ten. tenn forces word-reading (TTS read bare ten wrong, as if spelled out). A/B confirmed 2026-06-30 (option B). Whole-word \bten\b also catches ten-year-old / ten years.
ten.source: book canon the-war-for-paratua Ch 1, added 2026-06-24; A/B confirmed operator ear 2026-06-30

Erulias.note: PROPOSED eh-ROO-lee-us; King Erulias Tregarth (Ch 1 Order history); pending operator A/B confirm

Jublican.note: PROPOSED JOO-bli-kun; King Jublican Tregarth (Ch 1 Order history); pending operator A/B confirm

Toras.note: PROPOSED TOR-ahss; the Order's police-armies (Ch 1 Order history); pending operator A/B confirm

Ramatera.note: RAM-a-TEER-a, first-syllable stress (operator 2026-07-05; supersedes draft rah-mah-TAIR-ah)

lead-lined.note: HOMOGRAPH. The metal lead is /lEd/ (rhymes with 'bed'); the verb lead is /li:d/. They are spelled identically, so a bare 'lead' key would corrupt every verb use ('do not lead them to him'). Only the unambiguous COMPOUND is keyed. Bare metal uses must be reworded in prose ('leaden', 'the metal'); no lexicon can resolve them.
lead-lined.source: operator 2026-07-12 (the-war-for-paratua ch08 lead-lined room / ch10 lead-lined cases); EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (led-lined compound correct AND the verb "lead" verified untouched)

Lead-lined.note: HOMOGRAPH. The metal lead is /lEd/ (rhymes with 'bed'); the verb lead is /li:d/. They are spelled identically, so a bare 'lead' key would corrupt every verb use ('do not lead them to him'). Only the unambiguous COMPOUND is keyed. Bare metal uses must be reworded in prose ('leaden', 'the metal'); no lexicon can resolve them.
Lead-lined.source: operator 2026-07-12 (the-war-for-paratua ch08 lead-lined room / ch10 lead-lined cases); EAR-CONFIRMED by operator on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14 (led-lined compound correct AND the verb "lead" verified untouched)

Gia.note: JEE-ah (soft g, as in Gianni). Shedu; Atar's contact on the Plane of Air.

Netheron.note: HARD/VOICED th — the sound in 'the' and in the real English word 'nether' (nether region, nethermost). The hyphen is a WORD-JOINER, not stress notation: it hands Kokoro the real word 'nether', whose th English already voices, then joins 'on' so the name reads as one token. Without an entry the engine treats 'Netheron' as an invented string and defaults to the SOFT/voiceless th, which the operator rejected. Same technique as Atar -> 'Ate-tar'.
Netheron.source: operator A/B on Kokoro bm_george, 2026-07-14. NEW entry — Netheron had NO lexicon entry and was guessed by the engine in every render to date (47 uses in Book I). Found by a diagnostic sweep of all 12 invented th-names after the 'breethed' soft-th bug; Netheron was the ONLY one the operator ruled wrong — the other 11, incl. Nargathon (471 uses), were ear-confirmed correct as-is and deliberately have no entry.
